title: Playbook - Qualys WAS Findings, Scans, And Report Retrieval
applies_to: Retrieve Qualys WAS findings, scans, reports, or API-asset views for application security workflows
prerequisites: Correct tenant-specific Qualys API server URL
Clear decision on whether the workflow needs findings, scans, generated reports, or API-asset inventory
required_permissions: WAS product access for the target subscription
Visibility into the target web applications or API assets
Any report-download visibility permissions required by the account
request_sequence: Resolve the tenant platform and use the tenant-specific Qualys API server URL.
Authenticate using the Qualys auth mode available for the tenant.
If the need is current or trendable security data, search findings using POST /qps/rest/3.0/search/was/finding with criteria for webApp, tags, status, severity, or dates.
If the need is application inventory or API-asset scoping, search web applications using POST /qps/rest/3.0/search/was/webapp and include tag or TruRisk fields as needed.
If the need is scan execution history or scan-centric evidence, search scans using POST /qps/rest/3.0/search/was/wasscan and download scan results for specific scan IDs when needed.
If the need is a report artifact, search or locate reports with POST /qps/rest/3.0/search/was/report and download the chosen report via GET /qps/rest/3.0/download/was/report/<id>.
Normalize WAS findings separately from VMDR data; preserve webApp or API asset identifiers, finding status, severity, QDS, and detection dates.

confirmed_facts.fact: WAS findings are searchable at POST /qps/rest/3.0/search/was/finding.
WAS scans are searchable at POST /qps/rest/3.0/search/was/wasscan and scan results are downloadable.
WAS reports are searchable at POST /qps/rest/3.0/search/was/report and downloadable through the download report route.
The WAS APIs UI shows API assets with tags, open vulnerabilities, and TruRisk score, which makes API-asset views useful for application-oriented reporting beyond VMDR.

implementation_notes: Use Search Findings for data pipelines and metric extraction. Use generated reports only when the requested output is a Qualys-produced artifact.
For application-security reporting by tag, scope web applications or API assets first, then search findings inside that scope.
WAS supports trendable dimensions such as firstDetectedDate, lastDetectedDate, and timesDetected, but they should stay in a WAS-specific reporting model.
Keep API assets in mind when the user asks about application or API vulnerability metrics; those are not VMDR host assets.
common_failures: Confusing VMDR host assets with WAS web applications or API assets.
Trying to use report downloads when the use case needs queryable finding rows.
Using the wrong Qualys URL family or wrong tenant pod.
Treating WAS API assets as if they were the same object type as Global IT Asset Inventory assets.
